skill.name: manifest
skill.description: Install and configure the Manifest observability plugin for your agents. Use when setting up telemetry, configuring API keys, or troubleshooting the plugin.
skill.summary: Install and configure the Manifest observability plugin for Claude agents with step-by-step setup and verification.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill uses Claude CLI commands to install and configure the Manifest observability plugin - this is the intended functionality. Network calls go to the official Manifest service (app.manifest.build). API key handling includes explicit warnings against logging secrets. Filesystem access is limited to reading standard gateway logs for verification.

content.value_statement: This skill helps you install and configure the Manifest observability plugin for Claude Code agents, enabling telemetry and monitoring for your AI workflows.
actual_capabilities: Install the Manifest plugin via Claude CLI
Configure API keys and custom endpoints for Manifest
Verify plugin installation by checking gateway logs
Troubleshoot common connection and configuration issues
limitations: Only works with the Manifest platform (app.manifest.build)
Requires user to provide their own API key
Does not provide built-in alerting or dashboard creation

use_cases.title: Enable agent observability
use_cases.description: Set up telemetry for a Claude agent to monitor its behavior and performance over time
use_cases.target_user: Developers running Claude agents in production

use_cases.title: Connect to self-hosted Manifest
use_cases.description: Configure the plugin to connect to a company's internal Manifest instance instead of the cloud service
use_cases.target_user: Enterprise users with self-hosted observability infrastructure

use_cases.title: Debug plugin issues
use_cases.description: Troubleshoot connection problems or configuration errors with the Manifest plugin

prompt_templates.title: Basic plugin setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @manifest to set up observability for my agent.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to enable monitoring for their Claude agent

prompt_templates.title: Setup with custom endpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @manifest to connect my agent to my self-hosted Manifest instance at https://manifest.internal.company.com/api/v1/otlp
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to use a self-hosted Manifest instance

prompt_templates.title: Troubleshoot connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @manifest to check why my agent is not showing up in the Manifest dashboard
prompt_templates.scenario: User is having issues with the plugin connection

prompt_templates.title: Reconfigure API key
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @manifest to update my API key - the old one expired
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to rotate their Manifest API key

output_examples.input: Use @manifest to set up observability for my agent.
output: I'll help you set up the Manifest observability plugin. Let me guide you through this step by step.
First, I'll stop the gateway to avoid hot-reload issues...
Now I'll install the manifest plugin...
To complete setup, you'll need a Manifest API key. Here's how to get one:
1. Go to https://app.manifest.build and create an account
2. Click 'Connect Agent' to create a new agent
3. Copy your API key (starts with mnfst_)
Once you provide your API key, I'll configure the plugin and verify it's working.

output_examples.input: Use @manifest to check my agent status
output: Let me check the gateway logs to verify the Manifest plugin is running...
I found the plugin logs. The observability pipeline is active.
Your agent is successfully connected to Manifest!

best_practices: Always stop the gateway before making configuration changes to avoid issues
Use the default endpoint unless you specifically need to self-host
Check gateway logs first when debugging any plugin issues
anti_patterns: Do not skip the gateway stop step - it can cause configuration to fail
Do not use API keys that do not start with 'mnfst_' - they are invalid
Do not share your API key in plain text - it gives others access to your observability data

faq.question: What is Manifest?
faq.answer: Manifest is an observability platform that helps you monitor and analyze your AI agent's behavior, performance, and usage patterns.

faq.question: Do I need an account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to create an account at app.manifest.build to get an API key for connecting your agent.

faq.question: Can I use a self-hosted version of Manifest?
faq.answer: Yes, you can configure a custom endpoint URL if you self-host Manifest. The skill will prompt you for this during setup.

faq.question: What does the API key look like?
faq.answer: Manifest API keys always start with 'mnfst_'. If your key has a different format, it is invalid.

faq.question: How do I verify the plugin is working?
faq.answer: The skill checks the gateway logs for '[manifest] Observability pipeline active' to confirm successful setup.

faq.question: What if I get a 'connection refused' error?
faq.answer: Check that the endpoint URL is correct and reachable. If using the default endpoint, verify your network can access app.manifest.build.
